questionHtml: Describe the barriers that exist to hybridization between species.
markschemeHtml:
1.  courtship behaviour is often complex and unique to a species;
  2.  (at several stages in courtship ritual) rejection occurs if characteristic behaviour not exhibited;
  3.  species-specific egg fertilization;
  4.  compatibility of acrosome with vitelline layer;
  5.  inability of successfully attached wrong species sperm to develop continuity with egg membrane;
  6.  sterility of interspecific hybrids;
  7.  due to differences in chromosome number;

meta.title: Describe the barriers that exist to hybridization between species.
breadcrumbLabels: A4.1.10. Barriers to hybridization and sterility of interspecific hybrids as mechanisms for of preventing the mixing of alleles between species
